examples.text: لَهُمْ شَرَابٌ مِنْ حَمِيمٍ وَعَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْفُرُونَ
examples.english: For them will be a drink of scalding water and a painful punishment because they used to disbelieve.

senses.gloss: at a fairly or comfortably high temperature
close (e.g., friend, associate, confidant), bosom (e.g., buddy)

examples.text: وَلَا تَسْتَوِي الْحَسَنَةُ وَلَا السَّيِّئَةُ ۚ ادْفَعْ بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ فَإِذَا الَّذِي بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَهُ عَدَاوَةٌ كَأَنَّهُ وَلِيٌّ حَمِيمٌ
examples.english: And the good deed does not equal the bad: repel by that which is better, whereupon he who is inimical to you will be as if he were a devoted friend.

senses.gloss: warm, friendly, cordial (e.g., relations between two or more parties)

etymology.text: فَعِيل (faʕīl)-type stative-verb derived from the active participle from the verb حَمَّ (ḥamma).
